---
title: "Linear Programming"
subtitle: "Chapter 2 – Section II: Core Optimization Techniques"
author: "Troy Altus"
date: "2026-04-12"
---

## Introduction to Linear Programming

Linear Programming (LP) is one of the most widely used optimization techniques in Operations Research. It involves optimizing a linear objective function subject to a set of linear constraints.

This chapter covers formulation, geometric intuition, solution methods, sensitivity analysis, duality, and practical Python implementations using PuLP.

---

## Standard Form

Maximize \( c^T x \)

Subject to \( Ax \leq b \)
\( x \geq 0 \)

---

## Placeholder

This is a placeholder chapter. Full content with examples and PuLP code will be added soon.

```{python} 
print("Linear Programming chapter - placeholder")
```
